message.create_headsupdisplay.bind_mode: Right-click a Display Terminal to bind
message.create_headsupdisplay.link_set: Linked to Data Integration Core at %s
message.create_headsupdisplay.link_succe: Bound terminal to Data Integration Core
message.create_headsupdisplay.link_monit: Radar Monitor bound to Data Integration Core at %s
message.create_headsupdisplay.scan_radar: Radar Monitor found and linked at %s
No radar Monitor found within %d blocks
message.create_headsupdisplay.no_termina: No Display Terminal bound! Use LinkBlock to connect Data Integration Core -> Terminal first.
gui.create_headsupdisplay.upload_image.s: Select an image from the left
tooltip.create_headsupdisplay.image_plug: §8» §7Allows the Data Integration Core to process images
§8» §7Click 'Add Image' in the Core to upload images
§8» §7Uploaded images will be sent to the server
tooltip.create_headsupdisplay.radar_plug: §8» §7Allows the Data Integration Core to connect to radars
§8» §7Click 'Scan Radar' in the Core to connect nearby radars
tooltip.create_headsupdisplay.head_mount: §8» §7Allows you to view information from a linked Display Terminal
§8» §7Right-click+shift on a Display Terminal to link
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.title: Data Integration Core - Help
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line1: 1. Use Link Block to connect Core to Display Terminal (right-click Core -> right-click Terminal)
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line2: 2. Add redstone source: click 'Add Source' and place frequency items
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line3: 3. Select a source and click 'Send' to push to terminal
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line5: 5. Plugin slots: click 'Plugins' to open plugin slots
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line6: 6. Terminal list: bottom-left, select target terminal
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line8: 8. Removing plugins will DELETE terminal data, use with caution
gui.create_headsupdisplay.help.line10: 10. Scan Radar: scan nearby radar monitors
create_headsupdisplay.ponder.omni_core.t: This is the Data Integration Core, a unified receiver for the display network
Use a Display Link as an example to establish a connection with the Core
Establish a connection using a similar method to the Display Link
You can also establish a connection by placing them adjacent
Right-click to open the core interface, select a display, then click Send on the data
The terminal can now receive this information and display it
For more info, check the core's help screen
create_headsupdisplay.ponder.display_ter: In the display network, the Display Terminal converts data into images
But it won't render images on its own
Attach a Display block next to the terminal to render images
Equip the Head Mount Display to render images on your screen
However, since the Head Mount Display and Display blocks differ in size, it's not recommended to bind both to the same terminal
